title: github jcob-bd/notebooklm-mcp-cliを調べて、kernel/FUGUEに活用できるか検証せよ。/kernelで。
summary_text: `/vote` result: `APPROVE_WITH_FALLBACK` The current NotebookLM thin-adapter slice is ready to keep. The repo now has a context-safe adapter at [notebooklm-cli-adapter.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/scripts/lib/notebooklm-cli-adapter.sh#L1), matching design policy in [kernel-notebooklm-cli-adapter-design.md](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/docs/kernel-notebooklm-cli-adapter-design.md#L15), with unit coverage in [test-notebooklm-cli-adapter.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/tests/test-notebooklm-cli-adapter.sh#L1). Verification passed: `4/4` tests. Council evidence: - `Codex`: implemented adapter, approval gate, bounded receipt, resolve-only flow, and tests. - `GLM`: current-run lanes succeeded for scope/risk review and did not raise a blocking issue. - `Claude`: bridge-authoritative lane timed out in this run and failed over to `Copilot`. - `Copilot`: fallback sidecar returned non-blocking concerns only. Vote conditions: - Keep `nlm-visual-brief` as the first shipped skill. - Do not expose full NotebookLM artifacts into prompt context. - Keep rollout behind a kill-switch / feature flag. - Next implementation step is adding the repo-managed skill/manifest layer on top of this adapter. Lane evidence at finish: - `Codex`: active to completion - `GLM`: active to completion - `Claude`: blocked in current run via bridge timeout; fallback sidecar used instead
